violations.text: The following in-use utensils were observed improperly stored at the time of inspection... - Utensils were observed stored in a hot hold unit with stagnant water observed at 122 degrees Fahrenheit. PIC swapped out utensils and increased water temperature to above 135 degrees Fahrenheit. - The allergen ice cream scoop was observed stored in stagnant water in the dipper well with the faucet off. Water was turned on for proper storage at the time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 06/10/2025

The receptacle in the shared/women's toilet room is lacking a cover. A toilet room used by females shall be provided with a covered receptacle for sanitary napkins. New Violation. Correct By: 06/24/2025

Protective shielding is not provided for the lights over the back prep area counter. Light bulbs must be shielded, coated, or otherwise shatter-resistant in areas where there is exposed food; clean equipment, utensils, and linens; or unwrapped single-service and single-use articles. New Violation. Correct By: 06/24/2025

Working containers of dry ingredients are not labeled with the common name of the food. Working containers holding food or food ingredients that are removed from their original packages, such as flour, herbs, salt, sugar, and spices must be labeled with the common name of the food. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 06/11/2024

Utensils were observed stored in stagnant water (115 DF) between uses. PIC swapped out utensils and turned up heat on the unit. Utensils must be stored in water above 135 DF if in stagnant water. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 06/11/2024

There was no evidence that employees are required to report to the person in charge information about their health (illnesses, symptoms) as they relate to diseases that are transmissible through food. New Violation. Correct By: 06/06/2023
Cherries were observed stored uncovered in the cold-hold unit. Food must be covered to prevent contamination from the premises. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 06/06/2023

Wet wiping cloths are not stored in a chemical sanitizer between uses. Wet wiping cloths must be stored in a chemical sanitizer at the appropriate concentration. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 06/06/2023

Ice cream scoops were observed stored in stagnant water between uses. The person in charge relocated the ice cream scoops to the dipper well at time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 06/06/2023

Single-use articles (disposable spoons and cups) were observed stored on the floor. Single-use articles must be stored at least 6 inches off the ground. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 05/23/2023
The facility is lacking test strips to measure the concentration of the quaternary ammonium sanitizing solution for the 3-bay sink. New Violation. Correct By: 06/06/2023
